title: Distinguish historical and live root CLI authority tracking
goal: Implement #523 by making root CLI authority candidate output distinguish closed historical tracking from live actionable ownership.
Keep the change bounded to the contract/defaults/check/test surfaces named by the issue.
non_goals: Do not implement the extraction candidates themselves.
Do not refactor broad CLI authority behavior.
Do not add tracker-specific assumptions to shipped package behavior.

intent.outcome: compact root CLI authority output no longer implies closed historical issues are live owners
intent.constraints: keep provenance and live ownership explicit without broad CLI authority refactors
intent.latitude: may adjust contract schema/data, compact defaults projection, checks, and focused tests
intent.escalation: stop if the fix requires implementing candidate extraction work or adding provider-specific tracker semantics

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, close #523, ingest #545, then continue the refreshed backlog.
execution.proof: contract tooling, structured inventory, workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, and defaults output passed

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_interpretation.literal request: #523: Distinguish historical and live tracking in root CLI authority candidates.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Agents can trust compact defaults output without mistaking closed historical audit issues for current owners.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Make candidate tracking metadata explicitly historical/provenance or live/current, and enforce that distinction in checks/tests.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: python runtime boundary contract/schema, root CLI defaults projection, contract tooling checks/tests, planning state/execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "root_cli_authority or runtime_boundary or contract_tooling"; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py --strict
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: before changing root CLI authority semantics beyond candidate ownership/provenance fields
execution_bounds.stop before touching: candidate extraction implementation, unrelated CLI sections, provider-specific tracker integrations

stop_conditions.stop when: candidate tracking cannot distinguish historical provenance from live ownership without changing broader contract semantics
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the fix requires implementing extraction candidates or adding GitHub-specific behavior to shipped contracts
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: implementation needs files outside the declared contract/defaults/check/test scope
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: focused defaults and contract tooling tests cannot prove the distinction
